moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
moor: noun. An extensive waste covered with patches of heath, and having a poor, light (and usually acidic) soil, but sometimes marshy, and
moot: adjective. Subject to discussion (originally at a moot); arguable, debatable, unsolved or impossible to solve.
mor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Moro.
morro: noun. A round hill or point of land.
morrow: noun. The next or following day.
mort: noun. Death; especially, the death of game in hunting.
mot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Barí.
motor: noun. A machine or device that converts other energy forms into mechanical energy, or imparts motion.
mow: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Moi (Congo).
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
rom: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Romani.
room: noun. An opportunity or scope (to do something).
root: noun. The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rotor: noun. A rotating part of a mechanical device; for example, in an electric motor, generator, alternator, or pump.
row: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dela-Oenale.
tom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tombulu.
tomorrow: adverb. On the day after the present day.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
tow: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Jemez.
two: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 2.
woo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Manombai.
worm: noun. A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.
wort: noun. Now chiefly as the second element in the names of plants: a plant used for food or medicine.
wot: verb. To know (in the sense of knowing a fact).
oom: noun. An older man, especially an uncle. (Frequently as a respectful form of address.)
oor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oorlams.
oto: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Oto-Pamean languages.
roo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rotokas.
rootworm: noun. A worm that tunnels through the roots of young plants.
rort: noun. A scam or fraud, especially involving the misappropriation of public money or resources.
rowt: verb. Alternative form of rout To make a loud noise.
rtw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rathawi.
trm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tregami.
wroot: verb. Obsolete spelling of root (“to dig or burrow with the snout”)
